configuration.notes: Live smoke test only; not part of the primary comparison.

reviewers.title: Authorization rule violation: invites creation not restricted to administrators
reviewers.description: The new route for creating invites allows any user with a membership in the team to create an invite. It checks for membership but does not verify that the member is a team administrator as required by REQ-AUTH-1.

reviewers.title: Unauthorized team invites flow exists but relies on missing route guard context
reviewers.description: The new route for creating invites checks membership by fetching membership and returning 403 if not found, but this is implemented in a way that depends on an undefined context: user and teamId are used without shown extraction from the request, and the code snippet shows a direct await on findMembership and request.json() without clearly defined authentication/authorization middleware. If the route does not ensure that only team administrators can create invites, this could allow non-admin members to trigger invites. The explicit product requirement REQ-AUTH-1 states only team administrators may create invitations; the patch does not demonstrate enforcement for administrator role distinct from general membership.

reviewers.title: Authorization check for creating invitations missing explicit enforcement of REQ-AUTH-1
reviewers.description: The patch adds a route for creating invites but only shows a single authorization check by finding membership and returning 403 if not found. It does not explicitly enforce that only team administrators can create invitations; membership alone may not guarantee admin rights. There is no explicit role/permission verification against an administrator role before allowing createInvite.
